description_long: This monastery features a rectangular main structure with a green gabled roof and white walls, accented by rows of arched windows. The central section rises to support a cluster of five golden onion domes, each topped with ornate crosses, characteristic of Russian Orthodox architecture. The building includes multiple entryways, with a prominent front entrance and covered side passages connecting to smaller annexes. The windows are symmetrically arranged, and the roofline is punctuated by decorative cornices and pilasters. The domes are supported by white cylindrical drums, adding vertical emphasis to the otherwise horizontal massing. The overall appearance is grand yet harmonious, blending religious symbolism with functional monastic spaces.
description: A grand white monastery with green gabled roofs, golden onion domes, and rows of arched windows, featuring ornate crosses and symmetrical architectural details.
description_view_0: This view highlights the side elevation, showing the long row of arched windows, the main entrance, and the cluster of golden domes rising above the central section.
description_view_1: The front view emphasizes the central entrance, the symmetrical arrangement of the green roofs, and the five golden domes aligned above the main structure.
description_view_2: This side view mirrors the opposite elevation, displaying the extended wing, arched windows, and the domes prominently above the central block.
description_view_3: The rear view reveals a secondary entrance, the back annex, and the alignment of the domes, with the green roof extending towards the viewer.
